certificate.moduleName: Astro Subscriber Motorola Advanced Crypto Engine (MACE) - Security Level 2
certificate.caveat: When installed, initialized and configured as specified in Section 11 of Security Policy. No assurance of the minimum strength of generated SSPs
certificate.description: The MACE cryptographic processor is used in security modules embedded in Motorola's Astro family of radio systems products. It provides secure voice and data capabilities as well as APCO Over-The-Air-Rekeying and advanced key management.

rows: Astro Subscriber Motorola Advanced Crypto Engine (MACE)
Approved Mode at overall Security Level 2
Authenticated Encrypt, Decrypt for storing SSPs
[133] Sections 4 and 5.2 Asymmetric key establishment key generation using unmodified DRBG output
[133] Section 4 and Section 6.1 Direct symmetric key generation using unmodified DRBG output
[133] Section 6.3 Symmetric Keys Produced by Combining (Multiple) Keys and Other Data
Key Agreement Scheme Key establishment methodology provides 192 bits of encryption strength
Decrypt OTAR Key blocks encrypted with AES 256 bit keys. Key establishment methodology provides 256 bits strength
key unwrapping only; Key establishment methodology provides 256 bits strength

[IG 2.4.A] P25 AES OTAR. No Security Claimed. AES MAC is used as part of OTAR but is considered obfuscation. KTS encryption is performed on the OTAR key components using AES KW and decrypted using AES KW Cert. #A2264
Data Input Data Output Control Input Status Output
The main physical port provided by the MACE. It provides access to the majority of the supported interfaces.
Data Input Control Input Status Output
This interface provides the input and output to a Key Variable Loader (KVL).
This interface provides status output to indicate all power-up self-tests completed successfully.
The MACE is upgraded to new firmware.
The DRBG is seeded and initialized. Success/failure status.
Keys imported into the MACE. Success/failure status.
Keys imported into the MACE. Success/failure status.

Updated the User password. Success/failure status.
Keys imported into the MACE. Success/failure status.
Module HW version, version information, and FIPS status.
Successful authentication will allow access to the services allowed for User role.
The MACE is configured as requested. Success/Failure status.
Identity-based: The CO role is authenticated to the MACE over SSI interface with 10-digit hexadecimal number.

The strength of the authentication method is 1/1610. The MACE limits the number of 15 consecutive failed authentication attempts. 15 consecutive failed authentication attempts cause all TEKs and KEKs to be invalidated and the password to be reset to the factory default. The probability of a successful random attempt during a one-minute period is 15/1610.

Update the MACE firmware. Firmware upgrades are authenticated using a digital signature. The Program Update Public Signature Key is used to validate the signature of the firmware image being loaded before it is allowed to be executed.
AES Key Unwrap, AES Certs. #A2261 or #A2262, DRBG Cert. #2265
Imports keys to the MACE via a Key Variable Loader (KVL) in plaintext.
AES Key Unwrap (KTS), AES Cert. #A2260 AES Cert. #2264
Import, modify and query the keys.
Modify the currently active keyset used for selecting keys for encryption/decryption services.
Modify the current password used to identify and authenticate the User role.
CKG, AES [197] CBC Cert. #A2261 or #A2262 AES CFB-8 Cert. #A2260, DRBG Cert. #A2265
Zeroize selected key variables from the MACE.
Obtain status information about a specific key/keyset.
Perform a key agreement process to create an ECDH Shared Secret, and ECDH Public and Private Keys.
CKG DRBG Cert. #2265, KAS-ECC [56Ar3], Cert. #A2266, ECDSA Cert. #A655
Zeroize the KPK and all keys and CSPs in the key database and causes a new KPK to be generated. Resets the password to the factory default.
Provide module version, firmware version, FIPS status

Perform module self-tests comprised of cryptographic algorithm tests, firmware integrity test, and critical functions test. Initiated by module reset or transition from power off state to power on state.
Validate the current password used to identify and authenticate the User role.
Provide the history of error events.
Clears the history of error events.
Download configuration parameters used to specify module behavior.
Covered with a hard-opaque epoxy coating that provides evidence of attempts to tamper with the MACE.
Look for signs of tampering. Remove from service if tampering found.
Generated external to the MACE and installed during manufacturing.
Derived from the DRBG input per SP800-90Ar1.
FIPS 186-4 compliant ECDSA key generation, using the internal CAVP validated DRBG.
CKG - Symmetric key generated by internal CAVP validated DRBG.
EC Diffie-Hellman shared secret generation using the internal CAVP validated 56Arev3 protocol
Generated per SP800-133r2 (Section 6.3 #2) via XOR of 2 other keys (IDK ROM and IDK Block)
Stored in the volatile memory (RAM) in plaintext.
Stored in the flash in plaintext, associated by memory location (pointer).
Stored in the flash in encrypted, associated by memory location (pointer).
Electronically input, AES-256 CBC encrypted by the IDK Block and ROM using AES KTS (Cert. #s A2261,
Electronically input, AES-256 ECB encrypted by the BKK using AES KTS (Cert. #s A2261, #A2262)
Electronically input, AES-256 CFB-8 encrypted by the PEK using AES KTS (Cert. #s A2261, #A2262)
Electronically input in plaintext through the directly connected KVL.
Electronically input using SP800-38F AES key transport by the KEK or TEK using AES KW Cert. #A2264.
Electronically established through ECDH Key Agreement Process (SP 800-56A KASrev3, Cert. #A2266)
Zeroized by program update service by overwriting with a fixed pattern “0s”. *
Zeroized by module power cycle or hard reset by overwriting with a fixed pattern “0s”. *
Zeroized by the “zeroize keys” service by overwriting with a fixed pattern of “0s”.
Zeroized by the “zeroize all keys and password” service by overwriting with a fixed pattern of “0s”.
Zeroized by the “validate password” service by overwriting with a fixed pattern “0s”.
Zeroized by the “change password” service by overwriting with a fixed pattern “0s”.
Zeroized by the “module configuration” service by overwriting with a fixed pattern “0s”.
Zeroized by the “privileged APCO OTAR” service by overwriting with a fixed pattern “0s”.
Zeroized by the “import keys over KYLD interface” service by overwriting with a fixed pattern “0s”.
256-bit AES key used for voice and data decryption.
256-bit password hash stored in the non- volatile memory.
The Load Entropy service provides the security strength required for the random number generation mechanism
The MACE enters the critical error state. In this state, the MACE
stores the status into the internal flash memory and then halts all
further operation by entering an infinite loop. The operator may
correct this state by power cycling the MACE.
The MACE enters the firmware signature validation failure state.
In this state, the MACE halts all further operations by entering
the flash programming mode. The operator may correct the issue
by power cycle and/or re-flashing a new image.
The MACE fails an ECDSA PCT.
The MACE enters a temporary error state. The generated key is
not used, and the Module returns an error code (0x1) to the
operator. The key is discarded, and the process abandoned.
A digital signature is generated over the Boot Block and
Base firmware using RSA-2048 (Cert. #A5253) code when
it is built and the Drop-in algorithms code uses RSA-2048
(Cert. #396) and is stored with the code upon download
into the MACE. When the MACE is powered up, the digital
signature is verified. If the digital signature matches, then
the test passes, otherwise it fails.
AES-256 ECB encryption KAT - Inclusive to AES CBC and OFB
encryption mode testing with 256-bit key per IG 10.3.A.
AES-256 ECB decryption KAT – Inclusive to AES CBC and OFB inverse
function testing with 256-bit key per IG 10.3.A.
AES-256 ECB encryption KAT – Inclusive to AES CBC and OFB
encryption mode testing with 256-bit key per IG 10.3.A.
AES-256 ECB decryption KAT – Inclusive to AES CBC and OFB inverse
function testing with 256-bit key per IG 10.3.A.

AES-256 CFB-8 decryption KAT – Inclusive to AES-256 OFB testing with
AES-256 GCM encryption KAT as per IG 10.3.A,
AES-256 GCM decryption KAT as per IG 10.3.A,
AES-256 CTR_DRBG Health Tests (instantiation, generate, and reseed)
KATs performed before the first random data generation.
ECDSA P-384 Key Generation Pair-Wise Consistency Test
A digital signature is generated over the code when it is built using
SHA2-256 and RSA-2048 (FIPS 186-5). The digital signature is verified
Per IG D.F, separately tested KAS Shared Secret generation with P-384
RSA-2048 SigVer, performed before FW integrity tests. Inclusive for
SHA2-256 KAT, performed before FW integrity tests.
